title: Variable Importance, Heterogeneity Tests, and Visualization for Causal Forests
code: library(grf) library(UtopiaPlanitia)  set.seed(1995) n <- 500; p <- 5 X <- matrix(rnorm(n * p), n, p) colnames(X) <- paste0("X", 1:p) W <- rbinom(n, 1, 0.5) Y <- X[, 1] * W + rnorm(n)  cf <- causal_forest(X, Y, W)  # S3 summary: ATE, variable importance, heterogeneity tests summary(cf)  # S3 plot: diagnostics (default), or type = "rank", "pdp", "inter" plot(cf, type = "rank")  # LOCO variable importance vi <- cf_loco(cf) summary(vi) plot(vi)

title: Auto-Selected Nuisance Causal Forest — autocf
text: Refits grf::causal_forest() replacing conditional outcome m(x) = E[Y | X = x] propensity e(x) = E[W | X = x] best-performing nuisance estimator candidate pool, scored shared K-fold cross-fit weighted CV loss. CATE estimator identification assumptions grf unchanged — Robinson-residualisation nuisances swapped (winner beats grf's baseline configurable margin).

title: Auto-Selected Nuisance Causal Forest — autocf
code: autocf(   c.forest,   X = NULL,   Y = NULL,   W = NULL,   K = 5L,   seed = 1995L,   eps = 0.001,   tuning = c("orig", "cf.default", "cf.autotune"),   pool = c("grf", "glmnet", "xgboost", "tabpfn", "bart"),   min_improvement = "1se",   term_evals = 10L,   tabpfn_args = list(),   glmnet_args = list(),   xgboost_args = list(),   bart_args = list(),   verbose = FALSE,   ... )

title: Auto-Selected Nuisance Causal Forest — autocf
text: c.forest fitted causal forest object grf package, used recover (X, Y, W) (via c.forest$X.orig, c.forest$Y.orig, c.forest$W.orig) inherit tunable hyperparameters refit. X, Y, W Optional overrides design matrix, outcome vector, treatment vector. NULL (default) recovered c.forest. X must fully numeric: grf baseline (always present) final grf::causal_forest() refit require numeric design matrix, factor/character columns rejected — encode (e.g. one-hot via model.matrix()) fitting. NAs X tolerated: grf, xgboost, bart, tabpfn handle missingness natively; glmnet candidate auto-imputes via glmnet::makeX(na.impute = TRUE) (mean imputation), fit per training fold applied held-fold avoid leakage. NAs Y W allowed. K Integer >= 2. Number cross-fit folds shared across candidates. Default 5. seed Integer seed fold assignment, per-candidate RNG, downstream grf::causal_forest() call. Default 1995. eps Numeric (0, 0.5). Propensity clipping bound binary W (winner's predictions clipped [eps, 1 - eps] preserve overlap). Default 1e-3. tuning Character scalar controlling grf::causal_forest() tunable hyperparameters handled refit. One "orig" (default, inherit verbatim c.forest$tunable.params), "cf.default", "cf.autotune". User ... overrides win. pool Character vector candidate nuisance estimators compare. Default c("grf", "glmnet", "xgboost", "tabpfn", "bart"). candidate whose required dependency missing (incompatible non-trivial sample.weights) dropped warning. "grf" baseline always refit K-fold cross-fit protocol candidates (using grf::regression_forest() held-fold), CV losses apples--apples. "bart" candidate uses dbarts::bart() dbarts package defaults, parallel chains via dbarts::guessNumCores() deterministic seed. min_improvement Either character string "1se" (default), character string "0", non-negative numeric. Controls margin candidate must beat grf baseline trigger nuisance swap. "1se" requires mean fold-wise loss reduction exceed paired standard error fold differences (1-SE rule). "0" swaps improvement. numeric value swaps absolute loss reduction exceeds . term_evals Integer >= 0. Random-search budget xgboost AutoTuner (per outer fold per nuisance). 0 disables tuning uses mlr3's default xgboost hyperparameters. Default 10L. tabpfn_args, glmnet_args, xgboost_args, bart_args Optional named lists per-candidate extra arguments. tabpfn_args forwarded tabpfn::tab_pfn(); glmnet_args glmnet::cv.glmnet(); xgboost_args forwarded mlr3 xgboost learner's param_set$values (use set, e.g., nthread = 4 multi-core CPU device = "cuda" enable GPU systems CUDA xgboost build; defaults mlr3's reproducibility-oriented nthread = 1 CPU). bart_args forwarded dbarts::bart(); default adapter sets nchain nthread dbarts::guessNumCores() uses dbarts defaults everything else (ntree = 200, ndpost = 1000, nskip = 100, keeptrees = FALSE); user keys bart_args override . Tuned hyperparameters override matching keys. default list(). verbose Logical. Print per-candidate / per-fold progress. Default FALSE. ... Additional arguments forwarded grf::causal_forest() refit, overriding values inherited c.forest values implied tuning.

title: Auto-Selected Nuisance Causal Forest — autocf
text: grf::causal_forest object auto-selected Y.hat W.hat. S3 class unchanged. extra attribute "autocf_meta" records comparison selection: K, seed, eps, w_type, clipped, tuning tabcf() / glmcf(). pool Pool requested caller. pool_run Pool actually compared dependency filtering. scores Data frame mean weighted CV losses per candidate (y_loss, y_se, w_loss, w_se). fold_losses Long-format data frame per-fold per-candidate weighted losses diagnostics re-derivation 1-SE rule. winner_y, winner_w Selected nuisance estimator per role. swap_y, swap_w Logical flags indicating whether swap fired (TRUE) grf's baseline retained (FALSE). gap_y, gap_w Mean fold-wise loss reduction (positive = candidate beats grf). min_improvement threshold used.

title: Auto-Selected Nuisance Causal Forest — autocf
text: function . diagnostic + drop-replacement. grf's default nuisance estimators (regression forests Y W) usually fine always best. autocf() runs small candidate pool shared K-fold cross-fit protocol weighted CV loss (mean-squared error Y continuous W; Brier score binary W, identical MSE 0/1 outcomes), reports comparison table, swaps competitor beats grf baseline margin choose. grf baseline scored --bag. "grf" candidate scored fitted forest's --bag honest nuisances (c.forest$Y.hat / c.forest$W.hat) — predictions actually keep — rather refit per fold. Every candidate still scored strict K-fold OOF predictions. Per-fold weighted MSE grf baseline computed binning OOB residuals K folds, 1-SE comparison machinery unchanged. 1-SE rule swap. finite-sample CV noise non-trivial candidate produce random improvement particular seed. Default min_improvement = "1se" requires mean fold-wise loss reduction exceed paired-fold standard error reduction: $$\text{swap} \iff   \bar d_K - \widehat{SE}(\bar d_K) > 0,   \quad d_k = \text{loss}^{\text{grf}}_k - \text{loss}^{\text{cand}}_k.$$ kind conservativism glmnet's lambda.1se uses, generalized model selection. Use min_improvement = "0" force improvement fire, supply numeric absolute threshold. Weighted CV losses. c.forest$sample.weights non-trivial, training per-fold loss computation weighted, loss minimised matches chosen nuisances actually used downstream grf. Candidates accept training weights (currently tabpfn) dropped pool warning weights non-trivial. Selection-induced inference. Picking best M candidates CV loss model-selection step. benign grf::causal_forest() uses Neyman-orthogonal R-learner score second stage; small perturbations chosen nuisance invalidate sqrt(n) inference (Chernozhukov et al., 2018; Foster & Syrgkanis, 2019). Without orthogonality safe. Convergence-rate caveat. CV-loss minimization picks lowest finite-sample loss, best convergence rate. valid sqrt(n) downstream inference (e.g. grf::average_treatment_effect()), chosen nuisances must converge o(n^{-1/4}). default pool members (grf reg-forest, glmnet sparsity, xgboost boosting consistency, BART Rockova & van der Pas, 2020) believed adequate typical n; tabpfn formal rate proof treated exploratory downstream confidence intervals matter. title: Modified LOCO Variable Importance for Causal Forests — cf_loco
text: LOCO measures. Leave-one-covariate-(LOCO) importance drops covariate (group covariates) model, refits forest without , measures much worse individualized predictions become. large score means variable important predicting heterogeneous treatment effects; score near zero means forest can recover CATEs without variable. Debiasing. Naive LOCO biased refitting forest introduces retrain variance. implementation uses debiased estimator Benard Josse (2023): full-data refit used bias correction term, per-observation CATE re-estimation performed via forest weights rather raw OOB predictions. Computational cost. function refits p + 1 causal forests (one per dropped variable plus one full-data refit), hyperparameters original. large datasets many covariates, consider using screen argument reduce number refits. Normalization. normalize = TRUE, negative importance scores (can occur dropping variable improves predictions) set zero, remaining scores rescaled sum one. gives proportional importance interpretation discards directional information. Variable grouping. Highly correlated covariates can grouped dropped together. Use group..corr = TRUE automatic grouping via correlation threshold, supply manual list via variable.groups.

title: Omnibus Tests of Heterogeneity — omni_hetero
text: function combines five tests treatment effect heterogeneity, grouped Preferred (valid size) Heuristic categories: Preferred tests (valid Type error control): Sequential RATE (Wager, 2024): k-fold cross-validated test correct size (valid Type error). trustworthy test battery preferred formal inference. Calibration test (Chernozhukov et al., 2018): Regresses doubly robust scores forest's CATE predictions. "differential forest prediction" coefficient tests whether forest captures meaningful heterogeneity beyond ATE. Heuristic tests (useful screening, formal inference): High vs. low CATE, cross-fit (Athey Wager, 2019): Splits sample half, predicts CATEs half using forest trained half, median-splits compares \(ATE_{high}\) vs. \(ATE_{low}\) within held-half. Cross-fitting avoids winner's curse contaminates naive -data version (see grf PR #1502). OOB RATE, two-sided (heuristic): Uses --bag CATE predictions directly. Known anti-conservative (~30\ null nominal 5\ screening tool, formal inference. OOB RATE, one-sided (heuristic): One-sided version . Approximately valid direction heterogeneity pre-specified.

OOB TOC panel draws TOC(q) = ATE among top-q units ranked predicted CATE, minus overall ATE, 95% pointwise band reports AUTOC (QINI) summary grf::rank_average_treatment_effect().

Name primary covariate. y_var Character NULL. supplied, 2-way PDP (tile plot) produced; otherwise 1-way PDP (line plot). grid_size Integer. Number grid points per variable (default 50). 2-way PDP grid grid_size^2 points, moderate values recommended. n_max Integer. Maximum rows covariate matrix used averaging. nrow(X.orig) > n_max, random subsample drawn. Default 2000 1-way PDP 500 2-way PDP (PD averaging converges fast; pass n_max explicitly override). show_ate_region Logical. (default TRUE). trim Logical. 2-way PDP : mask grid points outside convex hull training data avoid extrapolation unsupported regions (default TRUE). Uses grDevices::chull(). Ignored 1-way PDP. x.limits x axis limits specified c() vector. Defaults range grid. y.limits y axis limits specified c() vector. Defaults range grid. color.var Character NULL. NULL, raw values used labels. color.lab Character NULL. Legend title grouping variable. Defaults color.var. xlab Character NULL. Custom x-axis label. Defaults x_var. num.threads Integer NULL. Number threads predict(). Passed directly grf::predict.causal_forest(). NULL (default) uses available threads. subgroup Logical. TRUE, ignore partial-dependence machinery instead plot doubly-robust (AIPW) subgroup average treatment effect observed value x_var (, y_var given, cell x_var x y_var cross), via grf::average_treatment_effect() subset =. Intended binary low-cardinality integer covariates. 1-way: points 95% CIs per level. 2-way: heatmap subgroup ATEs * cells whose 95% CI excludes 0. Levels sort(unique(.)) binning; level/cell units estimate warned dropped. TRUE, grid_size, n_max, trim, show_scatter, color.var, color.cat, color.lab, x.limits, num.threads ignored; show_ate_region (1-way reference band), y.limits (1-way ), xlab still apply. Interpretation. flat rank plot (CIs overlapping ATE band) suggests homogeneous effects. steep S-curve CIs separating ATE band suggests meaningful heterogeneity — units benefit much (less) average. color gradient reinforces : red points units harmed unaffected treatment, green points units benefit .

what: New function cf_perm() — PermuCATE variable importance
title: UtopiaPlanitia (development version)
text: cf_perm() implements PermuCATE (Paillard et al., 2025): conditional-permutation variable importance fitted grf causal forest, returning importance scores one-sided p-values confidence bounds. complements cf_loco() — permutes rather refits, lower variance, reports inference. loss = "R" (default, Robinson residual) uses forest’s Y.hat/W.hat; loss = "AIPW" uses grf::get_scores(). default light path scores supplied forest place (approximate, conservative SEs); cross.fit = TRUE opts K-fold refitting Nadeau-Bengio inference (R-loss ). Ships print/summary/plot methods.

what: plot_pdp() gains a discrete subgroup ATE mode
title: UtopiaPlanitia (development version)
text: New subgroup argument (default FALSE). binary low-cardinality integer covariate, subgroup = TRUE plots doubly-robust (AIPW) subgroup average treatment effect observed value x_var — points 95% CIs (1-way), heatmap subgroup ATEs significance asterisks x_var x y_var cross (2-way) — instead smooth partial-dependence curve. Subgroup ATEs come grf::average_treatment_effect(cf, subset = ...). subgroup = FALSE default reproduces existing behavior exactly.

title: UtopiaPlanitia (development version)
text: screen = TRUE now drops zero-importance covariates. Auto-screening keeps every variable non-zero split-frequency importance (previously: mean, -selected). interactive runtime prompt removed. New verbose argument (default TRUE) prints conditioning-variable correlation matrix refits warns covariate pair correlated |r| = 0.5. Set verbose = FALSE silence. warning suppressed variables grouped via group..corr variable.groups. Note: default verbose = TRUE, existing calls now emit matrix (applicable) new warning.

what: omni_hetero() exposes the Sequential RATE fold count
title: UtopiaPlanitia (development version)
text: New num.folds argument (default 5) controls number folds K Sequential RATE test (Wager, 2024). Must integer >= 3 greater sample size; 5 recommended. upfront sample-size warning now scales chosen fold count. summary.causal_forest() forwards ... omni_hetero(), summary(cf, num.folds = 10) works.

what: tabcf() gains repeated cross-fitting and clip control
title: UtopiaPlanitia (development version)
text: New R argument repeated K-fold cross-fitting. Nuisance predictions averaged R independent fold partitions cut Monte-Carlo variance. R = 1 (default) identical previous behavior. New clip argument controls binary-propensity clipping: FALSE (default; clipping, overlap warning), TRUE (clip [1e-3, 1 - 1e-3]), c(lo, hi) custom bounds. Behavior change: binary propensities longer clipped default. Previous versions always clipped 1e-3; default now warn-. Pass clip = TRUE restore old clipping. eps deprecated favor clip. maps clip = c(eps, 1 - eps) deprecation warning.

title: UtopiaPlanitia (development version)
text: loco() now accepts grf outcome forests addition ranger. Supported classes: grf::regression_forest(), grf::boosted_regression_forest(), grf::probability_forest(). Causal, survival, quantile, instrumental, multi-arm, lm_forest objects rejected informative error messages; causal_forest() error points users cf_loco(). Backend auto-detected model class — new argument behavior change existing ranger users. Hyperparameters replayed fitted grf object (tunable.params plus num.trees, ci.group.size, clusters, equalize.cluster.weights). grf::boosted_regression_forest(), boost.steps replayed length(model$forests); boost.error.reduction reverts grf’s default. sample.weights honesty flags preserved. data = ... ignored grf models one-shot warning; grf forests store training data internally. Split-mode grf models always uses internal custom split loop — split-sample inference machinery used every ranger split-mode case. New tests tests/testthat/test-loco-grf.R.

what: loco() gains classification DV support and group-LOCO
title: UtopiaPlanitia (development version)
text: Classification / probability forests now first-class. treetype guard relaxed: regression, probability estimation, (hard) classification ranger forests supported. Survival forests remain rejected. New loss argument ("auto", "abs", "mse", "brier", "zero_one", "log"). Defaults "auto" resolves "abs" regression, "brier" probability forests, "zero_one" classification forests. Disallowed combinations (e.g. "brier" hard-classification forest) raise informative error pointing user refit probability = TRUE. New groups argument group-LOCO. Pass NULL (default) per-variable behavior; pass character vector single unnamed group; pass named list character vectors multiple groups dropped jointly. Validates non-overlap, known names, non-emptiness, least one predictor remains drop. New members column appears output groups supplied; list-column character vectors naming group’s members. New loss column added unconditionally output downstream consumers can tell residual flavor produced row. Split mode dispatch: every split-mode configuration — per-variable regression loss = "abs", classification, probability, custom loss, group LOCO (groups supplied) — handled -package custom split loop (loco_custom_split()), implementing sample-splitting + one-sided Z Wilcoxon inference, generalized drop sets columns compute user-defined per-observation loss residuals.

previous_headings: loco() gains classification DV support and group-LOCO
what: Backward compatibility for the classification + group features
title: UtopiaPlanitia (development version)
text: default-argument call signature loco(model) unchanged. Existing per-variable regression callers see variable, importance, (ci.lower, ci.upper, p.value,) method columns; output diff new loss column (always "abs" existing regression calls).

previous_headings: loco() gains classification DV support and group-LOCO
title: UtopiaPlanitia (development version)
text: Brier-LOCO Type-rate classification null variables (n=250, trees=200, 20 reps, alpha=0.10, uncorrected): roughly 10-35%, comparable modestly better previously-documented regression -rejection. Brier-LOCO power true signal DGP: 0.95. Group-LOCO sanity: signal-group importance exceeds noise-group importance 20/20 reps regression DGP three signal three noise predictors.

previous_headings: loco() gains classification DV support and group-LOCO
title: UtopiaPlanitia (development version)
text: test-loco.R cases: probability-forest auto-loss (brier), probability-forest zero_one log losses, classification-forest auto-loss (zero_one), classification rejects brier/log, regression rejects brier/zero_one/log, probability rejects abs/mse, split-mode classification probability paths, multi-class probability across loss choices, MSE vs ABS loss differ, survival-forest rejection. Group-LOCO cases: OOB split output shape, singleton groups pred-name order equal per-variable LOCO, character vector becomes single group, validation errors (unknown names, overlap, empty group, drop-, duplicated group names), group classification forest, group + split + classification.

title: UtopiaPlanitia (development version)
text: New method = c("z", "wilcox") argument split = TRUE. tests computed package’s split-sample inference machinery (loco_custom_split()); Wilcoxon signed-rank variant robust heavy-tailed residual distributions default normal-theory Z-test. New bonf.correct argument (default TRUE match old hidden behavior) exposes Bonferroni correction explicitly. New data argument lets callers pass training frame directly. Use model fit scope model$call$data longer resolves. Output gains method column ("z", "wilcox", "oob") downstream code can tell inference path produced row. One-sided p-value semantics documented. split-mode p-value tests H0: importance <= 0 vs H1: importance > 0. Values near 1 mean “evidence importance”, “evidence zero importance”.

title: UtopiaPlanitia (development version)
text: Hyperparameters now recovered fitted model object. Previously loco() parsed model$call tried evaluate symbols parent.frame(), crashed model fit inside function hyperparameters bound local variables (common pattern). Core hyperparameters (num.trees, mtry, min.node.size, splitrule, replace, max.depth) now read directly model. Uncommon arguments still come call graceful warning fallback can’t resolved. OOB mode longer crashes training frame columns beyond predictors response. now refits via ranger’s x / y interface ignores extra columns. OOB mode now honors seed argument. Previously silently ignored, OOB importance non-reproducible across calls. original .Random.seed restored exit. Regression-guard. Classification, probability, survival forests now rejected informative error rather silently returning meaningless residual differences. Single-predictor models rejected early clear message instead crashing inside ranger. Factor predictors: allowed OOB mode (ranger handles natively); rejected split mode clear message split-mode LOCO requires numeric predictor matrix. x / y interface guard. Models fit ranger(x = X, y = y) dependent.variable.name previously silently failed deep inside train.data[[""]]. now error early workaround pointer.

title: UtopiaPlanitia (development version)
text: cf_loco already noted issue; loco() docs now also note naive Wald-style Z-test per-observation OOB squared-error differences anti-conservative (Type-roughly 30% nominal 10% checks) OOB residuals positively dependent across trees. Consequently add inference mode split = FALSE; users wanting p-values set split = TRUE. Documented finite-sample -rejection split-mode Z Wilcoxon tests applied random forests: checks (n = 200, p = 4, 20 reps, x4 noise) empirical Type-rate alpha = 0.10 roughly 50-75 percent, random forests can exploit noise feature slightly better generalization finite samples. tests read exploratory screens rather confirmatory inference.

title: UtopiaPlanitia (development version)
text: New tests/testthat/test-loco.R covering: data-frame shape sort order, seed reproducibility, extra-column handling, factor predictor handling, regression-guard, single-predictor guard, hyperparameter recovery, x/y interface guard, split-mode column contract, Wilcoxon method, Bonferroni monotonicity, CI-width monotonicity alpha, match.arg / alpha input validation.

title: UtopiaPlanitia 0.3.1
text: autocf() gains "bart" candidate via dbarts::bart(). Default pool now c("grf", "glmnet", "xgboost", "tabpfn", "bart"). Adapter runs dbarts defaults parallel chains (nchain = nthread = dbarts::guessNumCores()) deterministic seed; user overrides via new bart_args argument.

title: UtopiaPlanitia 0.3.0
text: New plot_pdp(): real Friedman (2001) partial dependence plots 1-way 2-way CATE surfaces. New plot_scatter(): renamed old plot_pdp(), plotted individual OOB CATEs vs. covariate. plot_inter() deprecated favor plot_pdp(c.forest, x_var, y_var). plot.causal_forest() gains type = "scatter" new plot_scatter().

title: UtopiaPlanitia 0.2.0
text: Added S3 summary() plot() methods causal_forest objects. Added S3 summary(), print(), plot() methods cf_loco output. Added plot.causal_forest() dispatcher plot_diag(), plot_pdp(), rank_plot(), plot_inter(). Fixed deprecated aes_string() usage across plot functions. Fixed bare namespace references plot_diag(), plot_rank(), plot_inter(). Replaced size linewidth geom_smooth() calls. Pruned 13 8 core exported functions; archived extras R/old/. Added GitHub Actions R-CMD-check, pkgdown, test coverage, linting. Switched license MIT GPL-3.

title: UtopiaPlanitia 0.1.0
text: Initial release cf_loco(), omni_hetero(), plot functions.
